_meta.title: scripts/expand_instruct_ko.hexa P2 stubs implemented
_meta.consumes_for: ALM A1 Φ-aware LoRA Korean instruction expansion

stages: exact: SHA-256(instruction.trim().substring(0,200) || output.trim().substring(0,200)) via temp file (handles Korean + quotes)
approximate: token 3-gram set Jaccard > 0.8 → reject as near-dup

convert_sample.signature: fn convert_sample(raw: SampleStd, fmt: string) -> ConvertResult
rejects: instruction.trim() empty AND output.trim() empty → ok=false
fmt not in {alpaca, chatml} → ok=false, format="unknown"
convert_sample.returns: ConvertResult { ok: bool, format: string, payload: string }

structs_added: SampleStd { instruction: string, input: string, output: string }
ConvertResult { ok: bool, format: string, payload: string }
stub_reason: These require HuggingFace `datasets` binding / Path / set primitives that are not in stage0 hexa. Out of P2 scope (P2 = unblock the data pipeline core; P3 = wire it to live datasets).

cases.name: alpaca_ok
cases.expects: alpaca payload contains instruction key, Korean text preserved, lang:ko

cases.name: chatml_ok
cases.expects: chatml payload contains messages array, role user+assistant, Korean text preserved

design_notes: stage0 hexa string indices are BYTE-based (not char); substring/index_of on multibyte content can mid-cut codepoints. Where mid-cut matters (dedup key cap @ 200 byte), we accept the approximate cut because SHA-256 still fingerprints uniquely.
json_stringify builtin works for primitives; we use a manual escape for nested struct → payload to keep ASCII-stable output across stage0 builds.
eik_sha256 writes through temp file rather than shell-quoting to handle Korean + quotes + newlines safely.
MinHash uses a deterministic Jaccard over token 3-gram set (no random hashing) — sufficient for in-batch near-dedup, deterministic for tests.
feedback_hexa_struct_list_alias respected: no struct contains an array field. Parallel arrays used in dedup_hash for hash/shingle bookkeeping.

verification.expected_double_println: stage0 quirk: top-level main() invocation duplicates output (use+main both fire). Test counters guard against double-counting.

next_p3_actions: wire load_ko_dataset to a hexa-native dataset reader (jsonl streaming) to bypass `datasets` python binding
build seen_hashes from existing train.jsonl/val.jsonl via dedup_hash inverse
auto-detect HuggingFace field maps via dict literal workaround (parallel array catalog)
promote dedup_hash to streaming O(1) memory using SHA-256 set + LSH bands
